refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operations to reduce energy, and optimizing system performance aligns with the energy efficiency KPI and supports verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and comparing to E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy; confirm control actions reduce energy use; demonstrate optimization algorithms improve efficiency.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system parameters and operational strategies to achieve energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors integrated with telemetry system for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive control algorithms in vehicle control system to reduce energy consumption.
DP3: Use data analytics and feedback loops in NGHE solution to optimize operational parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization aspects of energy efficiency.

potential_sources: DP2 depends on DP1 data accuracy.
DP3 depends on DP1 and DP2 outputs.
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but maintain clear functional boundaries; dependencies are manageable with proper interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data and reliable telemetry communication.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting vehicle safety and performance.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of data and model accuracy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing control and optimization to isolate complexity and facilitate safety validation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration of monitoring, control, and optimization functions for energy efficiency.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and load data transmission between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands from NGHE Solution to Vehicle for control adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone supporting data exchange among NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and Teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency logic and optimization; moderate diffusion due to telemetry and tasking interfaces.
Hosts sensors and control actuators; some diffusion due to embedded control algorithms.
Minimal involvement in energy efficiency functions.
Safety criticality high but no direct energy efficiency implementation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functions are best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize logic and minimize diffusion across modules.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution module may impact maintainability.
Interfaces with Vehicle must be robust to ensure data integrity and control command reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and modularize NGHE Solution internally to manage complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FRs are well-defined and mapped to logical DPs that address monitoring, control, and optimization aspects of energy efficiency.

diffusion_risks: Moderate diffusion between NGHE Solution and Vehicle due to telemetry and control interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within NGHE Solution is strong; interfaces are well-defined and limited to known communication channels.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy_per_ton and validating data flows and control actions through telemetry and tasking interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy efficiency functions to NGHE Solution with supporting roles for Vehicle sensors and actuators.

recommended_revisions: Modularize NGHE Solution to separate monitoring, control, and optimization internally.
Strengthen interface specifications to ensure data integrity and command reliability.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirements for progressing to detailed design and implementation planning. Interfaces and containment are well-defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system parameters and operational strategies to achieve energy efficiency target.

revise.why: To modularize NGHE Solution internally for better complexity management and maintainability.

assumptions: Sensor data and telemetry communication are reliable and accurate.
Control algorithms can be safely implemented within vehicle constraints.
open_risks: Complexity of optimization algorithms impacting system performance.
Potential interface failures affecting data integrity and control.
evidence_needed: Validation test results of energy_per_ton metric against E_target.
Interface performance and reliability test reports.
